mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS levels.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.5pp in 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which boosts corporate revenue forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with an interaction term coefficient insignificant at p < 0.10 in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost pressures and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases consistently precede negative 5-day SPX returns during supply normalization periods, with a negative Rank IC exceeding -0.04.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above 3% forces central banks to maintain restrictive monetary policy, which increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 20-day returns remain positive in > 70% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0% YoY.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising total employment and falling unemployment indicates a strengthening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction between PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline shows no predictive power for 60-day returns with a partial R-squared below 0.01 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth reflects robust aggregate demand and corporate profit expansion, which directly translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 levels show zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward SPX returns across all tested rolling windows with a Rank IC below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in industrial production signals immediate supply-side contraction and reduced economic activity, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances following a greater than 1.0% MoM drop in INDPRO.
